Exact_question: Which detection architectures achieve the highest robustness on 4K egocentric frames under practical compute constraints?
Final_finding: The seven-source error-hardened RF is the scientific balanced detector policy.
Claim_boundary: Scientific seven-source evaluation only; the App three-source runtime approximation has no assigned score.

Exact_question: How do GAN-based, diffusion-based, and standard perturbation face anonymisation methods compare with respect to privacy-utility trade-offs when applied to egocentric lifelog imagery under a standardised evaluation protocol?
Final_finding: No universal anonymiser wins; visual gates keep generative comparators research-only and deterministic methods default-eligible.
Main_metric: 500 locked frames; RiDDLE 0.903056 numerical comparator; 14/100 obvious-artifact inspection
Claim_boundary: Protocol- and objective-specific; no claim of complete anonymisation or universal method dominance.

Exact_question: Can a single multimodal pipeline protect privacy more completely by handling faces, visible text, and screens together rather than faces alone?
Final_finding: Multimodal handling extends coverage and residual completion closed held-out screen-presence misses while text/localisation/utility risks remain.
Main_metric: presence recall 0.9804; screen FN 0; adaptive redaction 0.8320 vs fixed 0.8319
Claim_boundary: Presence is not localisation; not complete PII removal and not statistically dominant over fixed redaction.

Exact_question: Can quality-aware adaptive method selection outperform fixed anonymisation strategies in this egocentric setting?
Final_finding: Scientific OAPR provides auditable objective- and condition-aware behaviour with safety gates and actual routed-output evidence.
Main_metric: 500 routes: 286 layered; 81 solid mask; 133 reviewed no-face copy
Claim_boundary: Bounded objective-specific value; competitive with strong fixed methods and not universally superior.
